Petition Re Financial Elder Abuse; Objections To Account; To Compel Trustee To Account; Breach Of Trust And Fiduciary Duty; For Surcharge, Fees And Da
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Continued To Mar-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andy Verriere appeared by zoom for Arlene Jeung (Trustee).
James Lamping appeared by zoom for Donna Ing.
Heather Hoekstra appeared by zoom for Brian Jeung and Rodney Jeung.

The Court stated that it received the joint case management statement and thanked the parties stating that it was very helpful. Mr. Verriere and Mr. Lamping stated that the petition for instructions can go off calendar. Mr. Lamping stated that they scheduled mediation for February 1st. Mr. Verriere stated that he heard from the Mediator who indicated that the date is not available, and that he hopes to get mediation done in early February. The Court addressed the party's positions that the issue of retrofit to the property can be decided without an evidentiary hearing leaving the Suisun and the cambridge account stating that all three issues are highly contested, and that the Suisun property and the retrofit issue seems to assume very similar facts. Mr. Verriere stated that the retrofit is related to a separate property other than the Suisun property, that the main issue is undisputed and that Paul Won was not financially damaged by the retrofit. The Court made reference to Probate Code 1022. Mr. Lamping stated that the crux of the claim is that the Trustee chose not to have the retrofit done because she knew it would take away from her and her family. The Court informed the parties that it will not decide the issue of retrofit. Mr. Verriere raised the issue of Ms. Ng's proposal of adding her children as petitioners stating that they have a dispute as to that, that he spoke with Mr. O'Sullivan and that it seems it would call for a motion. The Court informed the parties to do what they need to do, asked that they don't waste the Court's time and stated that it will not prejudge a motion. The Court also informed the parties that the matter will need to go out to Department 206 for trial, stated that it does not know what the trial format will be, suggested that the parties meet and confer, and advised the parties to continue on with mediation. The Petition re Financial Abuse, etc. is continued to March 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to provide an update on the ADR process. The Petition for Instructions and the case management hearing are off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Continued To Dec-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Re Financial Elder Abuse; Objections To Account; To Compel Trustee To Account; Breach Of Trust And Fiduciary Duty; For Surcharge, Fees And Da
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Continued To Dec-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Verriere appeared by telephone for Arlene Jeung.
Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared by zoom for Donna Ing (Petitioner).
Heather Hoekstra appeared by zoom for Brian Jeung and Rodney Jeung (Respondents).

The Court stated that it received the status report, that it was very helpful and asked if there have been any developments since then. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that there have been no developments, asked if the Court's view under the emergency order is that parties must submit to depositions remotely and stated that his Client wants to be in the room with him when being deposed. Mr. O'Sullivan also referred to CCP 2025 and asked if the Court will issue an order allowing more than seven hours (ten) for the depositions. Mr. Verriere objects to Mr. O'Sullivan's request. The Court stated that it wouldn't be likely to hold everything up because someone wants to have a semi live deposition, that the parties can agree on something, and authorizes eight and a half hours of depositions stating that video depositions take longer. The Court also advised the parties that it is issuing a case management order setting a case management hearing for December 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, that the order will require a meet and confer, and that it will set a trial date of March 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court also stated that the joint trial plan will determine if the trial will remain in Dept. 204, or be sent out to Dept. 206 and that Witnesses are not needed for the March 16th date. In addition, the Court raised the issue of the parties' position that mediation will be done before trial, but not right now. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that this is the consensus. The Court stated that at the next hearing it wants to hear when mediation will be and that it wants to have dates. The Court also stated that Arlene is to serve the case management order and that Mr. Verriere is to monitor the Court's register of actions and download a copy of the filed order and make sure it is served on all parties entitled to notice/service. The Petition Re Financial Elder Abuse, etc. and the Petition for Instructions are continued to December 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition Re Financial Elder Abuse; Objections To Account; To Compel Trustee To Account; Breach Of Trust And Fiduciary Duty; For Surcharge, Fees And Da
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Sep-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Continued To Jun-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Verriere for Arlene Jeung.
Heather Hoekstra for Brian and Rodney Jeung.
Ciaran O'Sullivan for Donna Ing.

The Court thank parties for the joint status report and found it helpful. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that all parties are getting discovery going, anticipate trial around October, and that parties can return in August for trial setting or mediation. The Court noted that based on the joint status report parties should return in mid-June to make sure everything is advancing with discovery. Mr. Verriere stated he hoped to get a trial date by the end of this year, around October, to control discovery cut-off. The Court noted it does not send cases to trial until it's ready.

The third amended petition re: financial elder abuse, etc. and petition for instructions are continued to June 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Joint status report to be filed by June 1, 2020 with courtesy copy delivered to Dept 202 by June 8, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition Re Financial Elder Abuse; Objections To Account; To Compel Trustee To Account; Breach Of Trust And Fiduciary Duty; For Surcharge, Fees And Da
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Continued To Jun-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Continued To Mar-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andy Verriere for Arlene Jeung.
Heather Hoeskstra for Rodney and Brian Jeung (Respondents).
James Lamping for Donna Ing (Petitioner).

Mr. Lamping stated that he is present in court against medical advice and he should be on bedrest until 1/20/20. The Court inquired if Mr. Lamping wished to continue the petitions. Mr. Lamping stated he can address some things. Ms. Hoeskstra stated she is objecting to Donna's 850 petition, that her clients were not actually personally served, petitioner needs to correct service and they are not waiving service. Mr. Lamping stated he will re-serve. The Court will hear Arlene's petition this morning. Ms. Hoekstra stated she is not objecting to Arlene's petition. Mr. Verriere stated there is one remaining item to be distributed in the Trust, that everything else was distributed, and the $1.4 million Cambridge account was never a part of the Trust. The Court noted the property was a specific gift to Donna. Mr. Lamping stated the Trustee can have earthquake retrofit work done through programs by the City and County of San Francisco, that the Trustee created a liability because Donna will be subject to personal liability if there are any injuries due to earthquake and they offered multiple options. The Court noted the issue is who should be holding this asset, and the answer is Donna should, he isn't sure if there was breach by any trustee, that they should look forward, and it is up to Donna to take or disclaim the property. Mr. Verriere stated they attempted to reach an agreement on resignation, which was unsuccessful, and seeking court approval if Donna disclaims the property then her children can either take or disclaim the property otherwise the property should be sold. Mr. Lamping stated this is the last property to be distributed then the Trust is done, there is an underlying litigation of other property that should be in the Trust. The Court stated Arlene remains Trustee until process is exhausted under the Trust and resignation can only happen with proposed successor or Trustee's acceptance.

After hearing, Counsel met and confer in hallway on proposed order for Donna to accept or disclaim property within 30 days. Mr. Lamping and Mr. Verriere presented the court with agreed upon language on a proposed order which is taken under submission.

The Petition for instructions on Questions of Trust Interpretation, etc., and Third Amended Petition RE: Financial Elder Abuse, etc., are continued to March 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Joint status report focused on evidentiary hearing, status of distribution, status on alternative dispute resolution, claims that can be decided without hearing, claims that need hearing, facts, proposed witness list and time estimates to be filed by February 24, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Robles. Not reported.

Petition Re Financial Elder Abuse; Objections To Account; To Compel Trustee To Account; Breach Of Trust And Fiduciary Duty; For Surcharge, Fees And Da
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Continued To Dec-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Continued To Oct-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Lamping for Donna Ing (Petitioner).
Andrew Verriere for and with Arlene Jeung (Trustee).

The Court noted that there are outstanding Probate Examiner's notes and addressed the request for consolidation stating that the petitions are filed in the same case number, and that it can't imagine that they will be tried separately. Mr. Lamping stated that he will file the notice of hearing next week. Mr. Verriere stated that he would like to distribute the trust property to Mr. Lamping's Client and raised the issue of liability to his Client regarding the rental property. Mr. Lamping stated that he objects to that, that there was an order from the City to retrofit the property, that Ms. Jeung should have done this when the Decedent was alive, that she held off because she knew it would lower her distribution, and that Ms. Jueng is trying to short fall his Client's petition and shift liability to his Client. Mr. Lamping also stated that penalties on the soft story retrofit start to accrue beginning in December, that the better solution is to have the retrofitting done and then distribute the property to his Client, that his Client is okay to have a lien against the property, that $1,400,000.00 was taken from the trust and that the Trustee has had this obligation since 2013. Mr. Verriere stated that they have met and conferred, and that they have gone round and round. The Court stated that the parties are to meet and confer as to how to get to resolution on both petitions, and that if it needs to be set trial, they need to discuss how long the trial will take and what needs to be done to be ready for trial. The Petition for Instructions on Question of Trust Interpretation, etc. and the Second Amended Petition Re: Financial Elder Abuse, etc. are continued to October 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition Re Financial Elder Abuse; Objections To Account; To Compel Trustee To Account; Breach Of Trust And Fiduciary Duty; For Surcharge, Fees And Da
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Continued To Mar-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Arlene Jeungs Notice Of Demurrer To Donna Ings First Amended Petition
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Verriere for Arlene Jeung (Respondent/Trustee).
Ciaran O'Sullivan for Donna Ing (Petitioner/Objector).

Matter called, argument held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with one modification: the reference as to Count Seven (Constructive Fraud) was stricken by the Court.

The tentative ruling as modified on Arlene Jeung's Notice of Demurrer To Donna Ing's First Amended Petition is adopted as follows: Count Eight (Fraud) of the First Amended Petition is sustained with leave to amend. Respondent is not correct that the elements of fraud cannot be pleaded on information and belief and a petition is subject to demurrer merely on those grounds alone. A fraud claim may be pleaded on information and belief so long as the facts upon which the belief is founded are stated in the pleading. The Petition states facts upon which Petitioner's belief is based. However, the Petition does not show a prima facie foundation for the fraud claim; there is no causal link pleaded between the alleged misrepresentation and the alleged damage to the Trust. The pleaded facts in support of the fraud claim also lack the specificity as to time and content exampled in Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Goods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, and Tenet Healthsystem Desert, Inc. v. Blue Cross of California (2016) 245 Cal. App. 4th 821. The amended petition is to be filed within 10 days from today. The order is signed in open court.

The March 11, 2019 hearing/status is continued to April 15, 2019.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.
